rationale: Two targeted fixes: (1) The severity description is too terse for mid-range calibration — expanding it with concrete landmark examples (noise/vibration/warning-light → info vs moderate vs urgent) reduces one-level misses. (2) The diagnosis_slug description doesn't strongly enough discourage over-diagnosing healthy vehicles; adding an explicit reminder to prefer no_fault_found when the described symptoms are normal/expected will reduce false positives on no-fault scenarios.

edits.old: severity: { type: "string", enum: SEVERITY_LEVELS, description: "info=no urgency, moderate=fix soon, urgent=fix now, do_not_drive=unsafe to drive" },
edits.new: severity: { type: "string", enum: SEVERITY_LEVELS, description: "Pick exactly one level: info=cosmetic or minor inconvenience, no safety/reliability impact (e.g. a squeaky trim piece, informational warning light with no drivability effect); moderate=noticeable symptom that should be repaired within days-to-weeks but poses no immediate danger (e.g. worn brake pads above minimum thickness, slow coolant seep, intermittent rough idle); urgent=active problem that risks breakdown or component damage soon — schedule same-day or within 24 h (e.g. brake squeal metal-on-metal, coolant temp rising, battery warning while driving); do_not_drive=immediately unsafe to operate (e.g. brake pedal to floor, steering loss, active fluid fire, structural failure). When in doubt between two adjacent levels, choose the lower one unless there is a clear safety or imminent-breakdown risk." },

edits.old: diagnosis_slug: { type: "string", enum: DIAGNOSIS_SLUGS, description: "the SINGLE best-matching root-cause slug from the controlled list. Match the vehicle's powertrain: use ev_* slugs (e.g. ev_battery_pack) for a fully electric vehicle and hybrid_* slugs for a hybrid — never use a hybrid_* slug for an EV or vice versa. Use no_fault_found if nothing is wrong, needs_further_diagnosis if genuinely indeterminate." },
edits.new: diagnosis_slug: { type: "string", enum: DIAGNOSIS_SLUGS, description: "the SINGLE best-matching root-cause slug from the controlled list. Match the vehicle's powertrain: use ev_* slugs (e.g. ev_battery_pack) for a fully electric vehicle and hybrid_* slugs for a hybrid — never use a hybrid_* slug for an EV or vice versa. IMPORTANT: if the described symptoms are normal vehicle behavior, expected wear at reported mileage, or no concrete fault is mentioned, use no_fault_found rather than guessing a specific repair — over-diagnosis wastes the owner's money. Use needs_further_diagnosis only when symptoms are real but genuinely ambiguous without hands-on inspection." },
